summary: Hand-authored parent-ID traversal so sync walks ClickUp's hierarchical resources (teams → spaces → folders → lists → tasks; teams → docs; teams → channels) instead of failing on the first list endpoint that requires a parent ID.
reason: The generator's default sync layer assumes global list endpoints (e.g. /users, /repos). ClickUp requires a parent ID for almost every list operation, so without parent traversal every hierarchical resource fails sync with `unknown sync resource` or HTTP 404. This is a generic hierarchical-API gap likely worth backporting upstream; in the meantime the traversal lives here.
validated_outcome: Default `sync` populates 116 records across 8 resource types in the printer's three workspaces (3 teams, 1 user, 6 spaces, 0 folders, 9 lists, 86 tasks, 2 docs, 9 channels).
